metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Students develop proficiency in two-dimensional art media including drawing, painting, printmaking, and mixed media while exploring art principles and diverse artistic traditions. This course emphasizes technical skill development, aesthetic exploration, and the ability to use visual language to express ideas and emotions. Students engage with colour theory, composition, and diverse creative approaches while developing their personal artistic voice.
metadata.general_description: Create sophisticated 2D artwork using drawing, painting, printmaking, and mixed media to develop artistic expression.
cross_curricular_connections: English Language Arts (visual literacy and interpretation)

big_ideas.statement: Two-dimensional artistic media provide diverse means for expressing ideas, emotions, and perspectives.
big_ideas.elaboration: Through drawing, painting, printmaking, and mixed media, artists communicate meaning and explore visual possibilities in ways unique to each medium and artistic approach.

big_ideas.statement: Mastery of 2D art techniques requires dedicated practice, experimentation, and development of technical skill.
big_ideas.elaboration: Artists build competence through focused practice with media and materials, understanding how technical control enables expressive possibilities.

big_ideas.statement: Understanding art principles—colour theory, composition, balance, contrast—guides effective visual communication.
big_ideas.elaboration: Knowledge of how visual elements interact and organize provides artists with tools to intentionally create compelling and meaningful artwork.

big_ideas.statement: Artistic practice is informed by engagement with diverse artistic traditions and contemporary art.
big_ideas.elaboration: Artists draw on historical and cultural traditions while responding to contemporary artistic conversations, developing informed and distinctive personal artistic voices.

competencies.raw: Develop drawing skills and explore drawing as a primary artistic medium
i_can_statements: I can draw with careful observation and create representational artwork
I can develop line work, shading, and value studies with technical proficiency
I can use drawing to explore ideas and develop concepts for artwork
I can experiment with drawing media and styles to develop versatility

competencies.raw: Create paintings that demonstrate technical skill and artistic expression
i_can_statements: I can mix colours accurately and apply paint with control and intention
I can build layered compositions and understand how paint application affects results
I can work in multiple painting styles and approaches
I can use painting to communicate mood, atmosphere, and artistic meaning

competencies.raw: Explore printmaking processes and create print artwork
i_can_statements: I can execute relief printing techniques with accuracy and artistic consideration
I can create screen prints or other printmaking approaches
I can understand how printmaking reversal and multiple editions affect artistic meaning
I can use printmaking to explore pattern, repetition, and design

competencies.raw: Create mixed media and experimental artwork using diverse materials
i_can_statements: I can combine multiple media and materials effectively in artwork
I can experiment with unconventional materials and approaches
I can create collage and assemblage work with thoughtful composition
I can take risks and innovate while maintaining artistic coherence

competencies.raw: Analyze 2D artwork and understand how elements and principles create meaning
i_can_statements: I can identify how line, shape, colour, and value work together in artwork
I can explain how composition and balance affect viewer experience
I can recognize how contrast, emphasis, and texture contribute to artistic meaning
I can articulate how medium choices influence artwork appearance and message

competencies.raw: Evaluate my own artwork and identify areas for continued technical and artistic development
i_can_statements: I can assess technical execution and identify areas for refinement
I can evaluate how effectively my artwork communicates intended meaning
I can recognize my developing artistic vision and progress as an artist
I can set meaningful goals for continued artistic growth

competencies.raw: Understand artwork within personal, cultural, and historical contexts
i_can_statements: I can recognize how personal experiences and identity inform artistic choices
I can understand artwork within cultural and historical contexts
I can appreciate how diverse artists address similar themes differently
I can reflect on artwork's relationship to social, political, or environmental issues

competencies.raw: Develop informed artistic judgment and understanding of contemporary art practice
i_can_statements: I can distinguish between technical skill and artistic intention in artwork
I can recognize innovation and originality in contemporary and historical art
I can appreciate diverse artistic approaches and aesthetics
I can evaluate how art functions in gallery, public, and community contexts

competencies.raw: Create finished artworks that demonstrate technical mastery and artistic vision
i_can_statements: I can complete artwork that demonstrates technical skill and intention
I can execute finishing techniques that enhance overall artistic impact
I can maintain consistency and coherence across bodies of work
I can present artwork professionally and appropriately

competencies.raw: Share artwork and articulate artistic vision and process
i_can_statements: I can display artwork in classroom, gallery, or exhibition contexts
I can write or speak about my artwork, explaining artistic choices and meaning
I can discuss artistic inspiration and development process thoughtfully
I can engage viewers through presentation and discussion of my work

competencies.raw: Document artistic process and development through sketch books and reflection
i_can_statements: I can maintain a sketch book or documentation of artistic exploration
I can photograph and organize artwork for portfolio purposes
I can write reflectively about artistic choices, experiments, and learning
I can track development and growth as an artist over time

competencies.raw: Engage in critique and feedback processes to strengthen artistic practice
i_can_statements: I can present work for critique in supportive classroom contexts
I can accept and incorporate constructive feedback on my artwork
I can provide thoughtful, encouraging critique to peers
I can engage in meaningful dialogue about artistic choices and interpretation

competencies.raw: Connect 2D art to other disciplines and explore interdisciplinary approaches
i_can_statements: I can integrate 2D art with photography, design, or multimedia projects
I can explore how 2D art relates to science, mathematics, or other disciplines
I can understand art's role in social justice, activism, or community work
I can create interdisciplinary projects that combine 2D art with other forms

competencies.raw: Study diverse artists and artistic traditions from various cultures and time periods
i_can_statements: I can analyze and learn from historical and contemporary artists
I can explore artistic traditions from diverse cultures and communities
I can identify stylistic innovations and influential artistic approaches
I can understand how artists are influenced by cultural context and artistic heritage

competencies.raw: Develop awareness of art careers and pathways for continued artistic growth
i_can_statements: I can research career opportunities in visual arts, illustration, design, and related fields
I can develop a portfolio that demonstrates my artistic capabilities and vision
I can understand business and professional aspects of art practice
I can engage with mentorship and professional development opportunities

competencies.raw: Build understanding of art's role in culture and develop personal artistic voice
i_can_statements: I can recognize art's power to communicate, challenge, inspire, and celebrate
I can understand my own aesthetic preferences and developing artistic voice
I can commit to ongoing artistic growth and creative exploration
I can contribute to building a community that values artistic expression and creativity

areas.name: Drawing Fundamentals and Exploration
topics: Observational drawing and representational accuracy
Line work, mark-making, and drawing media exploration
Shading, value studies, and tonal drawing
Perspective, proportion, and spatial representation
Gesture drawing and expressive mark-making
Drawing as ideation and artistic development

areas.name: Painting and Colour Theory
topics: Colour mixing, colour relationships, and colour theory principles
Painting media: watercolour, acrylic, oil, and tempera approaches
Paint application techniques and surface effects
Composition and balance in painting
Working with value, saturation, and temperature in colour
Development of personal painting style and approach

areas.name: Printmaking and Repetition-Based Processes
topics: Relief printing: linocut, woodcut, and carving techniques
Screen printing: design preparation and printing process
Lithography and other planographic processes
Understanding reversal and mirror imaging in printmaking
Edition and variation concepts in printmaking
Combining multiple printing techniques

areas.name: Mixed Media, Materials, and Contemporary Practice
areas.raw: Mixed Media, Materials, and Contemporary Practice
topics: Collage and assemblage techniques and conceptual approaches
Integration of found objects and unconventional materials
Mixed media combinations and experimental approaches
Installation and site-specific artistic practices
Digital integration and hybrid media art
Contemporary art practices and artistic innovation
